_meta.note: Numeric-ID files (e.g. 1083724334.jpg) are uploaded media from the WordPress era — we don't have name metadata for them in the extracted DBs because their attachment records live in il_postmeta._wp_attached_file under different paths. Named files (coverimg__masani.jpg) are referenced by the mid-era wp_author/wp_book/wp_periodicals tables but are NOT present on this drive — they're inside the unextracted wp-content/uploads/ subtree of the tar.gz backup.

authors.name: Minoo Masani
authors.brief: Minocher Rustom Masani was born on 20 November 1905 to a Parsi family with an outspoken journalist and writer father, Mr. Rustom Masani. He was brought up in Bombay. He went on to train himself to become a lawyer at various institutions like Elphinstone College in Bombay, and the prestigious London

authors.name: B R Shenoy
authors.brief: Professor Bellikoth Ragunath Shenoy Ph.D (1905–1978) was a classical liberal economist. Born on June 3, 1905 near Mangalore, Karnataka, Bellikoth Ragunath Shenoy was educated at Benares Hindu University (where he secured a first class first at the MA Economics Exam in 1929) and later at the Londo

authors.name: C Rajagopalachari
authors.brief: C Rajagopalachari (popularly known as Rajaji) was born on 10 December 1878 in the Madras Presidency. He completed his graduation in Arts from the Central College, Bangalore and went on to study law at the Presidency College from where he graduated in the 1897. Rajaji went on to join the Indian Natio

authors.name: A D Shroff
authors.brief: Ardeshir Darabshaw Shroff (1899-1965) was an eminent industrialist, banker and economist of India. A D Shroff was one of the architects of free India's banking and insurance business. He was amongst the earliest proponents of free enterprise in India.

authors.name: Nani Palkhivala
authors.brief: Nanabhoy "Nani" Ardeshir Palkhivala was born in 1920 in Bombay. He was one of India's most eminent jurist and an expert economist. His knowledge on matters related to the constitution and tax matters was unparalleled. Palkhivala's biggest success came in the form of his contribution to the  famous K

authors.name: D R Pendse
authors.brief: D R Pendse was born on 6 September 1930 in Pune, India. He has a Bachelor's degree in economics, statistics and mathematics from Pune University and a B.A. and an M.A. degree from Cambridge university. He is fondly called Dada Pendse by his friends and colleagues.

authors.name: M R Pai
authors.brief: Mangalore Ranga Pai (1931-2003) was born in Manjeshwar, a town in Kerala bordering Karnataka. He attained Canara High School and went onto complete his undergraduation from the Government College in Mangalore. He graduated from the prestigious Presidency College in Chennai with a BA (Honours) in pol

authors.name: Sudha R Shenoy
authors.brief: A native of India and daughter of prominent Indian economist Professor B. R. Shenoy, Sudha Shenoy (1943-2008) was an Honorary Associate in Economic History at the School of Policy, University of Newcastle, Australia and a lecturer for the same from 1986 to 2004. She attended the London School of

authors.name: Sauvik Chakraverti
authors.brief: Sauvik Chakraverti (1956-2014) was an award winning columnist and author whose books, monographs and columns lighted the path of modern Indian liberalism.

authors.name: S V Raju
authors.brief: S V Raju (1933-2015) was one of the most prominent Indian Liberals. He was closely associated with Swatantra Party, and was the chief editor of Freedom First for many decades.

authors.name: Sharad Anantrao Joshi
authors.brief: Sharad Anantrao Joshi (born 1935) is the founder of Shetkari Sanghatana and Swatantra Bharat Paksha party; and has served as a Member of Parliament.

periodicals.title: The Indian Libertarian
periodicals.brief: The Indian Libertarian was a fortnightly journal, which was first published in the year 1957 by R B Lotvala and edited by his daughter Kusum R Lotavala. The journal defines its philosophy by stating, “As the name Indian Libertarian signifies, we stand for libertarianism as against totalitarianism an

periodicals.title: Forum of Free Enterprise
periodicals.brief: The Forum of Free Enterprise (FFE) was set up in the year 1956 by A D Shroff. It was established with the idea of organizing like-minded people to counter the socialistic and anti-private sector propaganda of the Nehru government. His vision was to keep the organization non-partisan and non-politica

periodicals.title: Freedom First
periodicals.brief: Freedom First, the Liberal Monthly, now in its 60th year of publication, was founded by the late Minoo Masani, author and MP, at a time when free India was just five years old, World War II had ended and the Cold War had begun. As a participant himself in India’s freedom struggle he shared the visio

periodicals.title: Swarajya
periodicals.brief: Swarajya - a big tent for liberal right of centre discourse that reaches out, engages and caters to the new India. Swarajya is a 58-year-old independent media start-up. We are a new-age media company with a rich and storied legacy. In 1956, journalist Khasa Subba Rau with the patronage of C Rajagopl

periodicals.title: Swatantra Party
periodicals.brief: Swatantra Party was founded in the year 1959 by leaders opposing the socialistic policies of the Congress government. Some of the prominent leaders of the time including C Rajagopalachari, Minoo Masani and N G Ranga collaborated to form a liberal and conservative party. The party stood for market-ba

periodicals.title: Indian Liberal Group
periodicals.brief: The Indian Liberal group was founded on 10th November 1965. The founding members included Minoo Masani, Prof B R Shenoy, Mr H V R Iengar, Dr Fredie Mehta, Mr Khushwant Singh and Madame Sophie Wadia. One of the objectives of the Indian Liberal Group as defined by them is that “the ILG seeks to emphas

periodicals.brief: This page includes publications from miscellaneous sources.

periodicals.brief: Liberal Times was a quarterly journal published by Friedrich Naumann foundation (FNF) South Asia

av_youtube_links.title: How I Stopped Worrying and Learned to Love the Trade Deficit | Sudha Shenoy
av_youtube_links.author: Minoo Masani
av_youtube_links.description: Lecture presented by Sudha R Shenoy at the Ludwig von Mises Institute in Auburn, Alabama; October 26, 2006.

av_youtube_links.title: Sunil Bhandare on enlarging the "constituency of reforms" in India
av_youtube_links.description: Sunil Bhandare, Advisor, Tata Strategic Management Group discussing ways through which what he calls "constituency of reforms" could be enlarged.

av_youtube_links.title: Sunil Bhandare on his experiences with Indian liberal organisations
av_youtube_links.description: Sunil Bhandare, Advisor, Tata Strategic Management Group talks about his interactions and experiences with Indian liberal organisations like Forum of Free Enterprise, Indian Liberal Group and the Project for Economic Education.

av_youtube_links.title: Sunil Bhandare on private enterprise post 1991 reforms
av_youtube_links.description: Sunil Bhandare, Advisor, Tata Strategic Management Group talking about private enterprise in post-reform era, their skepticism and how they overcame it.

av_youtube_links.title: Sunil Bhandare on Getting Disillusioned With Socialism and His Journey to Become A Liberal
av_youtube_links.description: Sunil Bhandare, Advisor, Tata Strategic Management Group speaks about getting disillusioned with socialism and his journey to become a liberal.

av_youtube_links.title: S. Divakara on The Forum of Free Enterprise
av_youtube_links.description: S. Divakara, the Director-General of The Forum of Free Enterprise talks about the organisation he has worked most of his life at.

av_youtube_links.title: S. Divakara on Nani Palkhivala's Union Budget Commentaries
av_youtube_links.description: S. Divakara, Director-General of the Forum of Free Enterprise speaking about Nani Palkhivala's famous Union Budget commentaries. These commentaries used to be annually organised by the Forum in Bombay soon after Finance Minister's annual budget speeches.

av_youtube_links.title: Minoo Shroff on His Uncle A D Shroff
av_youtube_links.description: Minoo Shroff talks about his uncle A D Shroff.

av_youtube_links.title: Minoo Shroff on Nehru, Welfare and India's Promise
av_youtube_links.description: Minoo Shroff of the A D Shroff Memorial Trust talks about Nehru, welfare, entitlements and India's promise.

av_youtube_links.title: Minoo Shroff on A D Shroff Memorial Trust and the Forum of Free Enterprise
av_youtube_links.description: Minoo Shroff talks about A D Shroff Memorial Trust and the Forum of Free Enterprise.

av_youtube_links.title: Minoo Shroff on Doing Business in Nehru's India
av_youtube_links.description: Minoo Shroff talking about the challenges while doing business in Nehru's India.
